seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Toowoomba and South Trees. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Toowoomba vs South Trees: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Toowoomba and South Trees.
heading: South Toowoomba vs South Trees — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Toowoomba, Queensland and South Trees, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Toowoomba has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to South Trees (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Toowoomba or South Trees safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Toowoomba has a flood risk score of 28/100, while South Trees scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Toowoomba scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. South Trees scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Toowoomba or South Trees?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Toowoomba has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
